mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for interacting with the Rancher server.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0/rancher /usr/local/bin/

rm -rf rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0

```

> Download the Rancher CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Clean up the extracted files.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service

sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml

```

> Install RKE2 on the server.
> Enable the RKE2 server service to start on boot.
> Start the RKE2 server service.
> Set the kubeconfig environment variable.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the newly created cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Install Rancher Server
steps.description:
This step deploys the Rancher Server using Helm, which will manage the Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

helm repo add rancher-stable https://charts.rancher.io

helm repo update

kubectl create namespace cattle-system

helm install rancher rancher-stable/rancher --namespace cattle-system --version 2.14.0 --set hostname=rancher.example.com

```

> Add the Rancher Helm chart repository.
> Update the Helm repositories.
> Create a namespace for Rancher.
> Install Rancher with the specified version.
commands: helm install rancher rancher-stable/rancher --namespace cattle-system --version 2.14.0 --set hostname=rancher.example.com

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher Server
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rancher Server and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall rancher --namespace cattle-system

kubectl delete namespace cattle-system

```

> Uninstall the Rancher release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher Server
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade Rancher, including a backup and verification.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace cattle-system

helm upgrade rancher rancher-stable/rancher --namespace cattle-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get pods --namespace cattle-system

```

> Check the status of Rancher pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade Rancher to the specified version.
> Verify that the Rancher pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the kubeconfig file is not pointing to the correct cluster or the cluster's certificate is not trusted.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KUBECONFIG environment variable is set correctly and the certificate is valid.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image 'rancher/rancher:v2.14.0': Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Docker daemon cannot access the Rancher image repository.

**Fix:**
Check your Docker login credentials and ensure you have access to the rancher/rancher repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not responding in a timely manner.

**Fix:**
Check the health of the Kubernetes nodes and ensure they are all in a 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "management.cattle.io/v3"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the Rancher CRDs are not installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Rancher CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rancher/rancher/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-crds.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved multi-cluster management features, and enhanced security configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy APIs in favor of newer versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.13.x, ensure to backup your data and check for deprecated API usage.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in the 'Ready' state, and Rancher should be accessible via the specified hostname.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
